questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The purpose of analysis of financial statements is to determine the financial position and performance of the business.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It involves studying the financial statements of a company to understand its strengths and weaknesses.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The purpose of analysis is indeed to determine the financial position and performance, and this is achieved by studying the financial statements to understand the strengths and weaknesses.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Ratio analysis is a method of analysis.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It helps in comparing the performance of a company with its past performance and with that of other companies.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Ratio analysis indeed helps in comparing the performance of a company, making it a method of analysis.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Horizontal analysis is used to compare the financial position and performance of a company over a period of time.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It is a trend analysis, which helps in identifying the pattern of changes in financial position and performance.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Horizontal analysis is indeed used to compare the financial position and performance over time, and this is achieved through trend analysis.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Vertical analysis is not used to compare the financial position and performance of a company.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It is a percentage analysis, which helps in comparing the financial statements with a base year or with a standard figure.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Vertical analysis is indeed used to compare the financial position and performance, making assertion (A) false.
